how_to_reach: Phone (940) 565-2195 · email InternationalAdvising@unt.edu · Marquis Hall, Room 110, 1155 Union Circle #311067, Denton, TX 76203 · General office hours Mon-Fri 8am-5pm; immigration advising is by appointment or scheduled drop-in/phone hours (see the ISSS site). Source: https://international.unt.edu/international-students/index.html (verified 2026-06-20)
when_to_use: Anything affecting F-1 status, work authorization, SSN eligibility, or whether a benefit is safe for your status.

topic: legal questions / rights / immigration legal advice
how_to_reach: Free legal consultations for enrolled UNT students from State Bar of Texas attorneys · email StudentLegal@unt.edu · University Union, Suite 372, Denton, TX. Source: https://studentaffairs.unt.edu/dean-of-students/programs-and-services/student-legal-services/index.html (verified 2026-06-20)
when_to_use: Anything that needs legal judgment, including immigration legal questions. For low-income civil legal aid in the region, Legal Aid of NorthWest Texas (https://texaslawhelp.org/directory/lanwt) also serves Denton County.

contact_name: Your chosen bank's branch / new-accounts line
how_to_reach: Confirm the current document list on the bank's own international-students page before you go, e.g. Chase (https://www.chase.com/personal/banking/education/basics/bank-accounts-for-international-students) or Bank of America (https://info.bankofamerica.com/en/international/student-bank-account), or call/visit a local branch. (verified 2026-06-20)
when_to_use: Confirming current document requirements before opening an account in person.

how_to_reach: Phone 940-565-2648 · email deanofstudents@unt.edu · University Union, Suite 409, Denton, TX. Source: https://deanofstudents.unt.edu/resources/food-pantry (verified 2026-06-20)
when_to_use: Applying for hardship/emergency support, the food pantry, or other campus resources.
